tokens.text: And it came to pass when men
tokens.range: ruddy i.e. a human being (an individual or the species, mankind, etc.)
soil (from its general redness)
a daughter (used in the same wide sense as other terms of relationship, literally and figuratively)
properly, to bore, i.e. (by implication) to wound, to dissolve; figuratively, to profane (a person, place or thing), to break (one's word), to begin (as if by an 'opening wedge'); to play (the flute)
to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage
above, over, upon, or against (yet always in this last relation with a downward aspect) in a great variety of applications
the face (as the part that turns); used in a great variety of applications (literally and figuratively); also (with prepositional prefix) as a preposition (before, etc.)
properly, to cast together , i.e. increase, especially in number; to multiply by the myriad

1.struct: contains a main verb; prepositional phrase(s)

tokens.range: ruddy i.e. a human being (an individual or the species, mankind, etc.)
ruddy i.e. a human being (an individual or the species, mankind, etc.)
soil (from its general redness)
to say (used with great latitude)
properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal (often collective)
(absolutely) to create; (qualified) to cut down (a wood), select, feed (as formative processes)
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
properly, to stroke or rub; by implication, to erase; also to smooth (as if with oil), i.e. grease or make fat; also to touch, i.e. reach to
properly, to sigh, i.e. breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. (in a favorable sense) to pity, console or (reflexively) rue; or (unfavorably) to avenge (oneself)
as far (or long, or much) as, whether of space (even unto) or time (during, while, until) or degree (equally with)
a bird (as covered with feathers, or rather as covering with wings), often collectively
to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application
the face (as the part that turns); used in a great variety of applications (literally and figuratively); also (with prepositional prefix) as a preposition (before, etc.)
a reptile or any other rapidly moving animal
the sky (as aloft; the dual perhaps alluding to the visible arch in which the clouds move, as well as to the higher ether where the celestial bodies revolve)

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.
עוֹלָם — "eternal" / "everlasting" / "age-long." Like Greek αἰώνιος, debate centers on whether this is absolute timelessness or the span of an era.

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.
עוֹלָם — "eternal" / "everlasting" / "age-long." Like Greek αἰώνιος, debate centers on whether this is absolute timelessness or the span of an era.
רוּחַ — "spirit" / "Spirit" / "wind" / "breath." Genesis 1:2 is famously ambiguous: "the Spirit of God," "a wind from God," or "the breath of God" are all grammatically valid.
